items.title: Dow Closes at Record; S&P 500, Nasdaq Rebound After Tech Sell-Off
items.content: Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at a fresh record high. S&P 500 rose 1.2%, Nasdaq 100 surged 2.3%, and Dow gained 307 points. Tech shares rebounded sharply, led by Alphabet (+4.1%). S&P 500 Q1 profits increased 28% YoY, fastest pace since 2021.

items.title: US stocks close sharply higher; S&P 500 +1.2%, Nasdaq +2.3%
items.content: US stocks closed sharply higher on Monday, with S&P 500 rising 1.2%, Nasdaq 100 surging 2.3%, and Dow Jones gaining 307 points to finish at a fresh record high, as investors reassessed the AI trade.

items.title: Stocks Rebound Early, Awaiting Fresh Jobs Data
items.content: Though June has been a tough month, S&P 500 is just 3% off all-time highs. Some tech selloff attributed to quarter-end rebalancing by major market players.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Soars to 23,026 as Hong Kong Stocks Rebound
items.content: Hang Seng Index advanced 1.57% to close at 23,026.68 on Monday, posting strong recovery following prior session's sharp global technology selloff. Peace talk optimism and technology recovery drove gains.

items.title: HK Market Midday: Sharp Divergence; Hang Seng Tech Index Rises 1.4%
items.content: Three major Hong Kong stock indices moved in mixed directions. HSI down 1.19%, Hang Seng Tech Index up 1.40%. Semiconductor, robotics, and optical communication stocks stood out. SMIC climbing nearly 8%. Traditional sectors including gold, oil, coal declined. Baidu surged over 4% on AI chip unit IPO news.

items.title: China equities ended week lower; Hang Seng dropped 5.24%
items.content: China's CSI 300 and Shanghai Composite fell 1.48% and 1.55% last week. Hong Kong's Hang Seng dropped 5.24% amid broader weakness in technology stocks and profit-taking in AI-related shares.

items.title: US PCE inflation revised sharply higher by Fed
items.content: Fed revised PCE inflation sharply higher to 3.6% for 2026 from 2.7% in March forecast. PCE has been above 2% for 63 consecutive months — longest sustained overshoot since early 1990s. GDP forecast lowered to 2.2% for 2026.

items.title: BEA revision could nudge US core inflation lower
items.content: Bureau of Economic Analysis open revision window runs from Q1 2021 through Q1 2026. Revision will fold five years of changes into annual GDP update. Core PCE — the Fed's preferred gauge — could be revised lower.

items.title: US, Iran agree to halt strikes and meet this week in Qatar
items.content: US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other and plan to hold talks this week in Doha, Qatar. Technical negotiators will meet on Tuesday, June 30, to save the 14-point Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding. The US indicated a deal to suspend attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Oil prices rise as US and Iran reach deal to halt attacks
items.content: Oil prices rose with WTI above $70/barrel again. Brent crude for August delivery at $73.21. Military tensions around Strait of Hormuz continue to unsettle energy markets. JMIC announced widened route through the Strait on June 27.

items.title: China's Tech IPO Boom Is Back — AI and Chip Firms Lead the Charge
items.content: Chinese semiconductor company reported 1,688% net profit jump in Q1 2026, fueled by global surge in memory chip prices driven by booming AI demand. Baidu's AI chip subsidiary Kunlunxin reportedly preparing for blockbuster HK IPO valued up to $50 billion.

items.title: Hawkish market reaction — potential rate hike this year
items.content: BlackRock Investment Institute notes hawkish market reaction following Fed signals. Markets pricing possibility of rate hike this year as inflation remains sticky above target. Data as of June 25, 2026.
